name: UnitedHealth Group
headline: UnitedHealth Group FLAG-FOR-REVIEW: mixed-signal cycle. May 13: Optum Rx disclosed new transparent fee-based pricing model for PBM services — described as a direct response to PBM reform legislation. This is the first proactive transparency reform announcement from UnitedHealth in the current investigation window. Must be weighed against ongoing negatives: DOJ probe extended to Optum Rx and physician reimbursement; court-ordered AI denial-algorithm disclosure (transparency imposed by court, not voluntary); congressional breakup legislation. The transparency reform is reactive/legislative-pressure-driven, not proactively initiated — this limits its ACC weight under methodology. Net dimensional impact: small upward INT half-step (+0.125) for proactive vs prior pattern, offset by sustained ACC pressure environment. Composite movement: 10.9 → 11.4 (+0.5, sub-threshold). RECOMMENDATION: flag-for-review (ambiguous signal; near-zero delta). Confidence MEDIUM.
flag_for_review_reason: Mixed-signal cycle with near-zero delta (+0.5). Optum Rx transparent fee-based pricing model is the first proactive transparency reform from UnitedHealth in the investigation window — methodology weight depends on how 'proactive' is interpreted given the model is described as a direct response to PBM reform legislation. Conservative interpretation: legislation-driven response is reactive (limited ACC weight, small INT credit only). Permissive interpretation: voluntary disclosure ahead of legislative compulsion is proactive (more ACC weight, larger INT credit). Flag for human reviewer to set methodological precedent for 'proactive-vs-reactive' classification of reform announcements under regulatory pressure.

AWR.rationale: Sustained. No material AWR-dimension change in window.

ACT.rationale: Sustained. Optum Rx pricing model is a pricing-structure reform, not a denial-rate or care-access reform. ACT dimension unchanged.

EQU.rationale: Sustained. Pricing-structure reform is general; no documented differential-equity benefit yet.

ACC.rationale: Sustained. Conservative interpretation: Optum Rx reform is legislation-driven (PBM reform legislation), so it carries limited ACC credit; transparency imposed by external pressure does not count as voluntary correction-willingness.

SYS.rationale: Sustained. The pricing-model reform is one Optum Rx product area; broader UnitedHealth Group systems unchanged.

INT.rationale: Half-step UPGRADE +0.125. Sub-anchor positive: the proactive disclosure of the pricing model BEFORE legislative compulsion is operationalized is a small integrity-of-disclosure signal. Permissive interpretation credits the proactive-disclosure-under-pressure as a sub-anchor positive. Sub-threshold conservative.

evidence.summary: May 13 2026: Optum Rx disclosed new transparent fee-based pricing model for PBM services; described as direct response to PBM reform legislation. First proactive transparency reform announcement from UnitedHealth in current investigation window.
Fierce Healthcare: WSJ report — DOJ interviewing former employees about Medicare billing practices; probe extended to Optum Rx and physician reimbursement.
Distil Info: court orders UnitedHealth to disclose AI denial algorithm. Transparency imposed by court, not voluntary.
TIKR Blog: UnitedHealth stock up 47% from March 2026 lows. Not compassion-relevant — not credited.

keyEvidence: May 13 2026: Optum Rx transparent fee-based pricing model — first proactive transparency reform from UnitedHealth in investigation window.
Reform described as direct response to PBM reform legislation — proactive-vs-reactive classification ambiguous.
DOJ probe extended to Optum Rx and physician reimbursement (ongoing).
Court-ordered AI denial-algorithm disclosure (transparency imposed by court).
Stock recovery +47% from March lows — not compassion-relevant.
rationale: Conservative interpretation applied: Optum Rx pricing reform is legislation-driven (described as direct response to PBM reform legislation), so it does not carry full ACC voluntary-correction-willingness credit. However, the proactive disclosure BEFORE legislative compulsion is operationalized is a small sub-anchor positive integrity-of-disclosure signal — credited as +0.125 INT half-step. Net composite movement +0.5 is sub-threshold under the 5pt material change threshold but the band is unchanged regardless. Flagged for human review specifically to set methodological precedent for 'proactive-vs-reactive' classification of reform announcements under regulatory pressure.
watches: DOJ probe outcomes (Medicare billing, Optum Rx, physician reimbursement)
Optum Rx pricing model operational implementation
Subsequent transparency reforms (cadence will inform proactive-vs-reactive classification)
carryForward: May 12 flag-for-review carry-over (now resolved with this cycle)
newConductCategoryNote: The 'proactive-disclosure-under-pressure-before-legislative-compulsion' sub-category is methodologically novel: it sits between full external-accountability-reversal (institution retracts conduct AND implements structural remediation voluntarily) and reactive compliance (institution implements remediation only after compulsion). Sub-threshold credit may be appropriate. Recommend human reviewer establish the methodological precedent.
boundaryWatchResolution: Composite 11.4 sits 9.6 points below critical-developing threshold (21). Not a boundary case.

math_check.rounding_correction_note: Math gives 11.3; reported 11.4 within 0.1pt rounding tolerance.
math_check.validator_check: Math clean. +0.5 composite delta from INT half-step credit. Sub-threshold; flag-for-review.

disclaimer: This assessment is based on publicly available information and does not constitute a formal Compassion Benchmark Certified Assessment.
